title: BSP v5p3x clock output sequencing audit
observations: BSP and local code both gate the card clock, issue update-clock commands, and re-enable the card clock before transfers.
BSP v5p3x temporarily disables and re-enables clk_mmc while changing the module clock rate.
The local diagnostic branch uses mainline clock-rate programming plus timing mode/divider setup and then re-enables the card clock.
PIO reads, partition discovery, and read-only root mount already prove the card clock/signaling path is functional when IDMAC is bypassed.
reason: The BSP clock sequencing difference is card-signaling/rate policy, not a source-backed IDMAC descriptor memory access precondition.
next: Do not retest clock output sequencing as a descriptor-fetch fix without new evidence tying it to the SDMMC0 memory master.
